markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Fold Project Brain query/runtime mapping, citation, context compaction, discovery vs lookup, and QueryPlan into WP-009 paths. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: ProjectBrainBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ProjectBrainBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified ac666fe3; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

findings.title: prefer_context_pack_reuse is a passthrough with no effect on the produced plan
findings.evidence: project_brain.rs:70-103 returns prefer_context_pack_reuse in ProjectBrainPlanInputs but does NOT inject it into RetrievalRequest, and the planner has no context-pack-reuse field/branch (the hybrid route always includes a ContextPacks step regardless). The folded prefer-fresh-ContextPack-reuse intent does not change planning.
findings.fix: Thread the reuse preference into the request/plan so the planner can prefer/skip the ContextPack step, or document it as caller-only metadata.

claim_mismatches.claim: prefer fresh ContextPack reuse where appropriate
claim_mismatches.reality: represented as a passthrough boolean that does not influence the QueryPlan

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-139 ProjectBrainBridge requires a deterministic backend contract that maps a Project Brain query into the WP-009 retrieval pipeline, distinguishing authoritative lookup (a stable handle present) from discovery/synthesis, and signaling fresh-ContextPack reuse. The committed code implements exactly this: presence of an AuthoritativeHandle yields AuthoritativeLookup/FactLookup, absence yields DiscoverySynthesis/Summarize with hybrid allowed, feeding the real CheapestAuthoritativePathPlanner whose QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace persist to PostgreSQL (migration 0141) and the EventLedger. It aligns precisely with current Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md lines 7316/7318 and 2817.
findings: src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_retrieval/project_brain.rs:70-106 map_query is real deterministic logic: has_handle -> AuthoritativeLookup + QueryKind::FactLookup; no handle -> DiscoverySynthesis + QueryKind::Summarize + HybridPosture::Allowed. This implements the spec-required discovery-vs-authoritative-lookup distinction.

Spec alignment confirmed against CURRENT v02.194: .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.194/spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md:7318 ([ADD v02.178] Project Brain MUST distinguish discovery from authoritative lookup; known WorkPacket/identifier -> prefer direct/bounded; hybrid reserved for discovery) and :7316 (QueryPlan/RetrievalTrace/fresh ContextPack reuse MUST be backend contracts, not UI-only) and :2817 (cheapest-authoritative rule with recorded skip reason). The bridge satisfies these.

sub_rule_1 PASS: grep of project_brain.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred'/'pending MT-'/Unavailable returns no matches (exit 1). No deferred-live escape on the required behavior path.

sub_rule_2 PASS: the bridge produces the real planner::RetrievalRequest type (planner.rs:75); all fields resolve including the post-MT-140 catalog_entry: None (project_brain.rs:98), confirming the working-tree code compiles against the current substrate and is genuinely wired, not isolated. Durable authority is PG-backed downstream: storage/knowledge_retrieval.rs record_retrieval_trace writes knowledge_retrieval_traces (migration 0141) and emits an EventLedger aggregate (lines 93-148). The suite's real-PG proof is in tests/knowledge_retrieval_bridges_tests.rs with MemoryFixture/skip_if_no_pg. The MT-139 unit test itself is a pure-mapping assertion, which is correct because the bridge is pure glue with no storage of its own; the managed-resource proof lives in the planner/compiler substrate it feeds.

Imported types verified to exist: memory/retrieval_mode.rs:288 QueryKind::FactLookup/Summarize; planner.rs:41 AuthoritativeHandle::WorkPacketId/EntityIdentity; plan.rs RetrievalBudgets::default_bounded, DeterminismMode, RetrievalFilters. The bridge is exported via knowledge_retrieval/mod.rs:45.

Non-blocking (LOW, matches the recorded adversarial-review-v2 finding): project_brain.rs:96-104 prefer_context_pack_reuse is a passthrough boolean surfaced in ProjectBrainPlanInputs but not branched into the QueryPlan. This is NOT a code-falsity or spec violation: the actual fresh-ContextPack-reuse backend contract (build/reuse/refresh/freshness decisions + EventLedger receipts) is implemented in the sibling recorder context_pack_recorder.rs (MT-142, ContextPackDecision::Reuse etc.), so the spec 7316 'explicit backend contract' requirement is satisfied at the WP level. The boolean is caller-supplied preference metadata, consistent with the MT acceptance ('signal whether a fresh ContextPack should be preferred'), not a falsified plan branch.

Paperwork aside (non-blocking per ground rules): packet cites v02.192 anchors but the same section numbers resolve in current v02.194; lifecycle.handoff session fields are null. Neither affects code correctness.
evidence_checked: git show ac666fe3 (MT-139 implementation commit, 1 file +157)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
